verdicts.note: Evidence confirms this is Freshdesk, a customer support/helpdesk platform.
Multiple evidence URLs reference freshdesk.com and freshworks.com/freshdesk.
Evidence consistently describes Freshdesk as a customer support/helpdesk platform.

The one_liner claims 'several community MCP servers already available' but the evidence only shows a single NPM package (freshdesk-mcp). No evidence of multiple MCP servers is provided. The evidence does confirm REST API v2, Basic Auth with API keys, and self-serve trial access.
Evidence confirms HTTP Basic Auth using API key. The SDK documentation shows 'api_key' parameter and references 'basicAuth' as the authorization type.
Pricing page confirms 14-day free trial. Free Program article confirms availability for 6 months (not permanent as originally stated). Paid plans available for continued access.

Free Program article states it's 'available for free for 6 months' not permanent. The original note about '14-day free trial' vs '6 months' needs clarification. The Free Program article does mention 'API rate limit set to zero' indirectly by listing features but not API access.
Evidence confirms REST API v2 with broad coverage (tickets, contacts, companies, conversations, agents, knowledge base), JSON format, CORS support, standard HTTP verbs. SDK wrapper also confirmed.

The URL provided (https://www.npmjs.com/package/freshdesk-mcp) points to an NPM package, which is evidence of ONE community MCP server, not 'several'. The exists field should remain 'yes' but the one_liner overstates the quantity. No evidence of official Freshworks-maintained MCP.
Evidence supports 'buildable-with-caveats'. The Free Program article confirms API limitations on free tier, and paid/trial plans provide API access.
Free Program article confirms limited API access on free tier. Trial and paid plans are required for full programmatic access.
All evidence URLs are valid and support the claims made in the record.
0.75 confidence is reasonable given the comprehensive evidence, though some details about Free Program duration (6 months vs permanent) need clarification.
Flags are generally supported by evidence, though 'Multiple unofficial third-party MCP servers' overstates what evidence shows (only one NPM package provided).

revised.name: Freshdesk
revised.one_liner: Freshdesk is a customer support/helpdesk platform offering a broad, well-documented REST API (v2) secured via HTTP Basic Auth with per-agent API keys, self-serve during trial/paid plans, with at least one community MCP server available.

auth_methods.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using personal API key as username and any string (e.g. 'X') as password. Key found in agent profile settings.

revised.self_serve_notes: Freshdesk offers a free trial (standard 14-day mentioned in industry sources) and a Free Program for 6 months for 1-2 agents. The Free Program includes essential features but API access is restricted/limited. Continued full API use requires a paid plan.

api_surface.notes: REST API v2 covering tickets, contacts, companies, conversations, agents, groups, knowledge base articles; JSON in/out, supports CORS, standard HTTP verbs. Also has a JS SDK wrapper.

revised.main_blocker: API access is restricted on the Free Program (6 months, 1-2 agents); a paid plan or active trial is required for full programmatic access, and self-serve API key generation requires an agent account with sufficient permissions.

evidence.supports: Freshdesk REST API v2 documentation, Basic Auth via API key
JS SDK and Basic Auth details, domain + api_key usage
Pricing plans including free trial and Free Program
Free Program available for 6 months for 1-2 agents with limited features
REST API architecture, resources, rate limits, pagination details

flags: Free Program (6 months) has restricted API access; paid plans required for full API use
At least one community MCP server exists (freshdesk-mcp on NPM); no official Freshworks-maintained MCP identified
Rate limits vary significantly by plan tier
